points.text: Assign a monotonically increasing sequence number to each event on the server.
Clients track the last sequence received and ignore any event with a lower or equal number.
Maintain a short‑term deduplication cache (e.g., an LRU set) keyed by event ID to drop repeats.
Use idempotent handlers so processing the same event twice has no side effects.
Secure tokens with a nonce or timestamp and validate them on each reconnect; reject reused nonces.
Run WebSocket over TLS (wss://) and optionally sign messages with HMAC to detect tampering.
Assign each event a strictly increasing sequence number per client session; clients drop events with numbers ≤ last processed.
Include the sequence number in the WebSocket message payload and have the client acknowledge receipt to detect gaps.
On reconnect, the client sends its last processed sequence number; the server resends any missed events starting from the next number.
Use TLS (wss://) to encrypt the channel and protect tokens from eavesdropping.
Issue short‑lived JWT or opaque tokens bound to the client’s session; validate on each reconnect.
Add a nonce or timestamp to each token and reject any token older than a configurable window to prevent replay.
Maintain a server‑side deduplication cache keyed by token or event ID for a limited time to drop duplicates.
Enable WebSocket ping/pong keep‑alives to detect dead connections promptly and trigger reconnection.
Use TLS (wss://) for all WebSocket traffic to ensure confidentiality and integrity of messages.
Attach a monotonically increasing sequence number to each event; server discards duplicates or out‑of‑order messages.
Include a per‑connection nonce (e.g., JWT jti) and reject any message with a reused nonce to prevent replay.
Issue short‑lived signed tokens (JWT) bound to the connection ID; verify signature, expiration, and audience on each reconnect.
Store recent token IDs (jti) in a cache with TTL and reject any token seen before to enforce one‑time use.
On client reconnect, require a fresh token; invalidate the previous token to stop replay of stale credentials.
Implement application‑level ACK/NACK so the client can resend missed events and the server can detect gaps.
